title: Lenovo IdeaPad Yoga 13 13.3-Inch Convertible 2 in 1 Touchscreen Ultrabook (Gray) 2013 Model

I bought this from an Amazon seller as a factory refurbished unit a few months ago.  It was in a "like new" condition when I received the unit. I have really enjoyed this 2-N-1 laptop.  I am sorry to say that I dropped my Yoga a few weeks ago and broke the charging port.  I have ordered some parts and am trying to repair.  I think Lenovo has a solid hit the Yoga models. Loved this laptop for the first six months. The hinges worked great and the cross-functionality between tablet and laptop was fantastic. However, after about six months of use I began to notice that the power cord and DC power jack were poorly constructed. The prongs in the jack stick out and completely broke off from the laptop during regular use. Needless to say, I was extremely disappointed.<br
keyboard_failure: I've had the Lenovo Yoga 13 for 1.5 yrs. I really like the form factor, display, performance, and weight. However, just a couple days ago some of the keys stopped working. I spoke with Lenovo and they said I need to pay a couple hundred dollars to repair this (since it's out of warranty). If I try to repair this myself I can order a keyboard for $50 and swap it. I'll probably go for the latter, bu

I've had my Yoga for almost two months. It is by far the best notebook/laptop I have ever owned. I especially enjoy the keyboard. I find myself picking up my Yoga rather than setting in front of my desktop when I need to prepare a long document.
The performance is excellent and the form factor is really comfortable.
My only complaint is that the battery life has been much less than what

I bought this about 18 months ago and loved it immediately it came out of the box. What I loved was the look rather than what I had bought. Windows 8 was horrible and the laptop from the start was never as fast as I was expecting. The keyboard is poor, after a couple of months the keys 9 & 0 stopped working. Looking on line found a fix that said if you turned off sleep control that would solve the
hinge_failure: I bought this in February 14th 2013 and the monitor no longer works.  I bought the top of the line product from them I-7 processor and a Solid State Hard Drive.  I paid over 1500 for this laptop thinking I would be well taken care and I would have had this laptop at least the next 3 to 4 years.  30 Days out of warranty the computer has lost its ability to transmit the information to the monitor.
I had this Lenovo Yoga for one day before a screen hinge broke.  They are not designed well at all.  I am very bummed because I really liked everything else about it.

My title says it all. The right hinge broke after 4 months and since I read the reviews about broken hinges I was very careful using them. The worst problem is the wireless reception. We bought 2 Lenovo Yoga computers for our twins last Christmas. bought it for my 13 year old daughter for school.
Lasted 8 months then the hard drive crashed.
Very disappointing. Would not recommend.

Decent computer but hard drive crashed after 3 months of limited use.
